title: Authentication, Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Privacy · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Resource Authorization
summary: User IDs arrive in headers, handlers authorize by route presence, shared source data leaks private follows, audit logs capture tokens and reading history, and deletion scope is unclear. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go resource authorization increment in tenant-safe identity and privacy boundary. Learners progress through predi…
objectives: Authorize each command from principal, tenant, role, ownership, relationship, and current resource state inside the application boundary.
Verify configured token issuer, audience, algorithm, time, key rotation, and transport before constructing a minimal principal.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Resource Authorization · Theory Feed Go Resource Authorizat…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 6222 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove verified identity, resource authorization, tenant query scope, privacy, and redacted audit evidence; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
